generated_oml:
// C3: Physical sensing components
instance InputCurrentSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Sensor measuring the robot joint input electric current"
    DTDFVocab:producedData CurrentRawTransmission
]

instance MotorAngularVelocitySensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Sensor measuring motor angular velocity of the robot joint"
    DTDFVocab:producedData AngularVelocityRawTransmission
]

instance FirstAxisPositionSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Sensor measuring the first‑axis (joint) position"
    DTDFVocab:producedData PositionRawTransmission
]

// C4: Physical-to-virtual interaction (data transmitted)
instance CurrentRawTransmission : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom InputCurrentSensor
]

instance AngularVelocityRawTransmission : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom MotorAngularVelocitySensor
]

instance PositionRawTransmission : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom FirstAxisPositionSensor
]

// C10: Data (after preprocessing)
instance FilteredCurrentData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Low‑pass filtered current measurement after preprocessing"
    DTDFVocab:fromData CurrentRawTransmission
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DigitalTwinProcessingEngine
]

instance AngularVelocityData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Motor angular velocity data (post‑filtering)"
    DTDFVocab:fromData AngularVelocityRawTransmission
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DigitalTwinProcessingEngine
]

instance AxisPositionData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Joint position data (post‑filtering)"
    DTDFVocab:fromData PositionRawTransmission
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DigitalTwinProcessingEngine
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance FrictionWhiteBoxModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "White‑box analytical model of joint friction (physics‑based Simulink simulation)"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DigitalTwinProcessingEngine
]

instance FrictionNeuralNetworkModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Black‑box neural‑network model for joint friction, retrained online with sensor data"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DigitalTwinProcessingEngine
]

// C11: Enablers
instance DigitalTwinProcessingEngine : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Processing component that consumes sensor data and models, runs simulations, updates models, and triggers services"
    DTDFVocab:enables JointFrictionMonitoringService,
                     PredictiveFaultDetectionService,
                     ModelUpdateService,
                     FrictionCompensationService,
                     MachiningParameterOptimizationService,
                     ProductQualityPredictionService,
                     VisualizationService
]

// C6: Services
instance JointFrictionMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Real‑time monitoring of joint friction using filtered sensor data and models"
    DTDFVocab:provides JointFrictionInsight
]

instance PredictiveFaultDetectionService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Predictive fault detection based on friction trends and model predictions"
    DTDFVocab:provides FaultDetectionInsight
]

instance ModelUpdateService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Automatic updating of both physics‑based and neural‑network friction models with new data"
    DTDFVocab:provides ModelRetrainingAction
]

instance FrictionCompensationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Active compensation of internal joint friction via the robot control loop"
    DTDFVocab:provides FrictionCompensationAction
]

instance MachiningParameterOptimizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Simulation‑based optimisation of machining parameters to reduce CO₂ emissions"
    DTDFVocab:provides MachiningParameterInsight
]

instance ProductQualityPredictionService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Downstream service predicting product quality using the central data memory"
    DTDFVocab:provides QualityPredictionInsight
]

instance VisualizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Visualisation of digital‑shadow data on ThingsBoard dashboard"
    DTDFVocab:provides VisualizationAction
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance JointFrictionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight into current joint friction levels for monitoring"
]

instance FaultDetectionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight indicating potential faults derived from friction anomalies"
]

instance ModelRetrainingAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Action to retrain the neural‑network friction model online"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance FrictionCompensationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Action to apply friction compensation commands to the robot control loop"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance MachiningParameterInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight on optimal machining parameters to minimise emissions"
]

instance QualityPredictionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight predicting product quality based on twin data"
]

instance VisualizationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Action to update ThingsBoard visualisations"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The twin operates in real‑time, receiving a continuous data stream from the robot via MQTT; the processing (e.g., friction model simulation) must respect the control system’s dead‑time, i.e., latency lower than the control loop period, enabling sub‑cycle updates. Synchronization is therefore continuous with granularity at the control‑loop cycle level."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The system follows a pipeline architecture: sensor data are streamed over MQTT to ThingsBoard, the AAS Executor extracts measured, simulated, and reference values and triggers simulation models (SysML structural model and Simulink friction model via FMI). The resulting outputs are written back to the digital shadow in ThingsBoard and visualised on a dashboard. Orchestration and resource management are handled by the AAS‑based component that automatically builds, runs, and updates the digital shadow."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "The DT lifecycle follows the MBSE abstraction hierarchy described in Document 1: (1) requirements engineering, (2) functional architecture modeling, (3) logical architecture modeling, and (4) product/technical layer (physical implementation). Each phase is represented with a specific model language – SysML for requirements and functional views, ADL (e.g., ACME, AADL, MontiArc) for component‑connector structures, and simulation models (Simulink, DEVS) for behavior at the physical layer. Transition between phases is managed by traceability links defined in the Digital Shadow Reference Model (Document 2) that associate data traces and processing components; conformance relations map concrete models to the reference model, providing governance of the lifecycle."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "The twin’s fidelity is maintained by coupling model verification, transformation traceability and continuous validation against live sensor data. Model transformations (e.g., SysML → Simulink) are verified for correctness as required by the Digital Shadow methodology, ensuring that the analytical white‑box models preserve the semantics of the source architecture. Traceability links from each data trace to its originating processing component and underlying model allow systematic error detection and correction. Uncertainty arising from model approximations or sensor noise is mitigated by the adaptive update loop described for friction modelling: new measurements continuously recalibrate parameters of the white‑box simulation or retrain the black‑box neural network, thus quantifying and reducing prediction error. Quality‑assurance processes include comparison of simulated versus measured values, verification of model‑based transformations, and the use of metadata to record provenance and confidence levels for each data trace."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The system relies on a lightweight MQTT publish‑subscribe network (Mosquitto broker) with hierarchical topics that can be permission‑controlled for read/write access. Sensors publish JSON‑encoded measurement messages to the broker; the AAS Executor and ThingsBoard subscribe to the same topics. The network topology is essentially a star/central‑broker model linking the physical sensors, the preprocessing unit, the MQTT broker, the AAS Executor, and the ThingsBoard dashboard. No explicit bandwidth, latency, redundancy or security protocol details beyond MQTT’s standard TLS support are provided in the documents."
]

instance horizontal_integration : DTDFVocab:HoriIntegration [
    base:desc "Integration with external systems is realised through Industry 4.0 standards: MQTT for IoT data transport, ThingsBoard for storage and visualisation, the Asset Administration Shell (AAS) for semantic wrapping, and the Functional Mock‑up Interface (FMI) to embed Simulink behavioural models with SysML structural models. These mechanisms provide interoperability with other enterprise systems and enable coupling with additional digital twins."
]
